title: Assessment Items - Crowd Gathering Control and Management

items.question: What is the first required action when a crowd gathers at the healthcare facility?
choices: Allow them to enter until leadership arrives.
Prevent unauthorized entry into the facility and associated premises unless official instructions are received.
Immediately disperse the crowd by force.
Ignore the situation if no damage is visible.
items.rationale: The first procedure is to prevent the crowd from entering unless instructions authorize otherwise.

items.question: After controlling initial access, what should security determine next?
choices: The financial cost of the incident.
The exact reason behind the gathering and whether it may escalate.
Which media outlet arrived first.
Which department is most crowded inside the hospital.
items.rationale: Security must investigate the causes of the gathering to understand the situation and its risk level.

items.question: What is required regarding staffing during a crowd incident?
choices: Reduce the number of security staff to avoid attention.
Deploy sufficient security personnel and establish security perimeters if required.
Send all staff inside the building and close the gates.
Wait until the crowd increases before taking action.
items.rationale: Adequate staffing and perimeter formation are part of the official response.

items.question: Who must be informed during the incident?
choices: Only the reception desk.
Only maintenance services.
Facility leadership and specialized security authorities through approved channels.
Patients' relatives first.
items.rationale: Leadership and specialized authorities must be notified without delay.

items.question: What restriction applies to members of the gathering around the facility?
choices: They may photograph freely if they stay outside.
They may raise banners if they remain peaceful.
They should not be allowed to photograph or raise banners in the facility surroundings.
They may do anything as long as they do not enter the building.
items.rationale: Photography and banner display are not allowed around the facility in this situation.

items.question: How should security coordinate with specialized authorities during the event?
choices: Only contact them once at the beginning.
Maintain continuous communication and coordination throughout the incident.
Wait until the crowd disperses before updating them.
Use informal messages only if needed.
items.rationale: Continuous communication is explicitly required during the incident.

items.question: What information must security document about the crowd?
choices: Only the names of hospital staff nearby.
The number of individuals, their classification, and the findings reported to security administration.
Only video footage from mobile phones.
Only the time of day.
items.rationale: The procedure requires counting, classification, and reporting.

items.question: What should happen if some individuals are allowed entry to meet responsible officials?
choices: They enter without any supervision.
An adequate number of security staff should accompany them under controlled access.
They should be redirected to public waiting areas without notice.
All crowd members should be allowed in together.
items.rationale: Authorized entry must remain controlled and accompanied by security personnel.

items.question: What is the correct rule regarding the use of force?
choices: Force should be used early to end the gathering quickly.
Force may be used freely if the crowd is loud.
Force should be avoided except in very limited cases permitted by law.
Force is required whenever photography occurs.
items.rationale: The policy restricts force to narrow lawful situations only.

items.question: When does monitoring of the crowd situation end?
choices: As soon as the crowd stops speaking loudly.
After one warning is issued.
Only when the situation is fully resolved and the crowd has dispersed from the surrounding area.
Immediately after leadership is informed.
items.rationale: Monitoring continues until the situation is over and dispersal is completed.

items.question: What post-incident documentation is required?
choices: No report is needed if the crowd leaves peacefully.
A joint report with specialized security authorities submitted to facility leadership.
A short verbal summary only.
A maintenance request form.
items.rationale: A joint report is required after the incident concludes.

items.question: Which communication style is most appropriate when dealing with a gathering?
choices: Use provocative language to pressure the crowd.
Refer to past incidents to create fear and gain compliance.
Demonstrate patience and calmness, show good conduct, and avoid provocative verbal or physical expressions.
Use threatening body language to establish authority.
items.rationale: The guidance emphasizes patience, calmness, respectful conduct, and avoiding provocation or fear.
